stories.title: Claude Code is steganographically marking requests
stories.titleZh: Claude Code 正在以隐写方式标记请求

comments.author: meowface
comments.textRawHtml:
Value judgment aside: I am a bit surprised at how sloppily they did this. I think they could've achieved the same effect while decreasing the odds of detection via reverse engineering.

(This field is known as "underhanded code", coined by the Underhanded C contest: https://www.underhanded-c.org. It's a little-known "art"; little-known for probably self-explanatory reasons. There are much cleverer ways of achieving objectives like this. One obviously being you can move more out of the client and into the server, but the other being you can write plausibly deniable client code in a much more benign-seeming way than this. Some of what they added can only be done on the client, but I think some could've been moved, and the client-required parts could've been done more subtly and credibly.)

It's possible they knew the JS bundle gets so heavily scrutinized that it'd eventually get spotted and reported on regardless so they didn't bother doing something more subtle and duplicitous. But still seems slightly lazy.
comments.textZhHtml:
抛开价值判断不谈：我对他们这样做的草率感到有点惊讶。我认为他们可以达到相同的效果，同时通过逆向工程降低检测的几率。

（这个领域被称为“卑鄙代码”，由 Underhanded C 竞赛创造：https://www.underhanded-c.org。这是一门鲜为人知的“艺术”；由于可能不言自明的原因而鲜为人知。实现这样的目标有很多更聪明的方法。显然，你可以将更多的东西移出他们添加的一些内容只能在客户端上完成，但我认为有些内容可以被移动，并且客户端所需的部分可以更巧妙、更可信地完成。）没有费心做一些更微妙和口是心非的事情。但还是显得有点懒。

children.author: superfrank
children.textRawHtml:
It's also possible that there are more in-depth detection methods and that this was just a cheap and easy first step that hasn't been removed because it catches a lot of less sophisticated bad actors.

It's unlikely that this will stop a big AI lab from distilling their model if they're really determined, but A) it may be enough to stop a bunch of fly-by-night token resellers looking to make a quick buck and B) you never know when one person at one of those big labs will mess up and forget to install whatever workaround they have and out themselves.

I think of it like if you have a problem with birds in your yard so you go buy one of those plastic owls. The owl scares away most of the birds, but not all of them, so you go and buy some ultrasonic noise thing to scare them away (I'm just making something up). Just because you bought the new ultrasonic thing though, that doesn't mean you're going to take the owl down. You leave it up because now you've got two layers of defense instead of one.
children.textZhHtml:
也有可能存在更深入的检测方法，而这只是一个廉价且简单的第一步，尚未被删除，因为它捕获了许多不那么复杂的坏人。

如果大型人工智能实验室真的下定决心，这不太可能阻止他们提炼他们的模型，但是A）这可能足以阻止一群想要快速赚钱的不可靠的代币经销商，B）你永远不知道什么时候会有一个这些大型实验室之一的人员会搞砸，忘记安装他们拥有的任何解决方法并自行退出。

我认为这就像如果你对院子里的鸟有问题，那么你就去买一只塑料猫头鹰。猫头鹰吓跑了大部分鸟，但不是全部，所以你去买一些超声波噪音的东西来吓跑它们（我只是编造的）。不过，仅仅因为您购买了新的超声波设备，并不意味着您会打倒猫头鹰。您将其保留，因为现在您有两层防御而不是一层。

children.author: meowface
children.textRawHtml: I'm sure they've had complex server-side detections for a while. But for the client parts: it should only contain the parts that must be on the client, and it could be done in a more benign-looking way. For example, the unavoidable client parts could've been done more fuzzily/broadly, for plausible deniability, and then narrowed on the server. (They may already have been following that strategy before now, without being noticed.)
children.textZhHtml: 我确信他们已经进行了复杂的服务器端检测一段时间了。但对于客户端部分：它应该只包含客户端上必须存在的部分，并且可以以一种看起来更良性的方式完成。例如，不可避免的客户端部分可以更加模糊/广泛地完成，以实现合理的否认，然后在服务器上缩小范围。 （他们之前可能已经在遵循该策略，但没有被注意到。）

children.author: weare138
children.textRawHtml: But what's stopping someone from modifying or injecting code into the client to bypass this 'restriction'? This type of security should be implemented server-side and at the network perimeter not in the client, especially one written in TypeScript.
children.textZhHtml: 但是是什么阻止某人修改或向客户端注入代码以绕过此“限制”呢？这种类型的安全性应该在服务器端和网络外围实现，而不是在客户端实现，尤其是用 TypeScript 编写的安全性。

children.textZhHtml: 我认为他们的想法是，_普通_用户会使用CC客户端，但通过第三方经销商，而经销商会拦截数据用于蒸馏（或其他用途）。如果要求用户使用修改过的客户端，或者除了简单地更改服务URL之外还要做更多事情，那会给那些只想更便宜地使用CC的人增加很多阻力

stories.title: Android Developer Verification: Threat masquerading as protection
stories.titleZh: Android开发者验证：伪装成防护的威胁

comments.author: sambuccid
comments.textRawHtml:
It doesn't solve the current issue, but in case we don't manage to push back on this, some people might not know that there are various actual linux OSes for mobile:

- SailfishOS: still linux based and seems fairly community inclusive, but the UI part of the stack is closed source. Is the only one officially allowed to run android apps, via emulation. Has existed for a very long time, it's lightweight and I think the most stable/bug-free in this list.

- Ubuntu Touch: fully open source and community driven, it uses snap packages for security, you might be able to run android apps. Last time I run it also seemed fairly stable/bug-free.

- PureOS: fully open source and privacy focused. I think it's the only one that, released with the Librem 5, can avoid using proprietary blobs for interfacing with the hardware. Seems less stable than SailfishOS and Ubuntu Touch. You would need to buy a fairly expensive-but-old phone(librem 5) to run it.

- PostmarketOS: fully open source, focused on being lightweight and revive old phones, has a huge amount of phones it has been tested on, is based on Alpine.

- Mobian: mobile version of Debian, it's fairly new on this list.

There are many more linux mobile OSes, but as far as I know these are the main ones. There might also be some inaccuracies on this post, I tested some of these a long time ago, and I never actually run the last 2.
comments.textZhHtml:
它并不能解决当前的问题，但万一我们无法对此进行反击，有些人可能不知道有各种实际的移动 Linux 操作系统：

- SailfishOS：仍然基于 Linux，似乎具有相当的社区包容性，但堆栈的 UI 部分是闭源的。是唯一官方允许通过模拟运行 Android 应用程序的应用程序。已经存在很长时间了，它很轻量，我认为是这个列表中最稳定/无错误的。

- Ubuntu Touch：完全开源和社区驱动，它使用 snap 包来保证安全，你也许能够运行 Android 应用程序。上次我运行它时看起来也相当稳定/无错误。

- PureOS：完全开源且注重隐私。我认为它是唯一一个与 Librem 5 一起发布的、可以避免使用专有 blob 与硬件连接的产品。似乎不如 SailfishOS 和 Ubuntu Touch 稳定。你需要购买一部相当昂贵但旧的手机（librem 5）来运行它。

- PostmarketOS：完全开源，专注于轻量级和复兴旧手机，有大量手机经过测试，基于 Alpine。

- Mobian：Debian 的移动版本，在这个列表中相当新。

还有更多的 linux 移动操作系统，但据我所知这些是主要的。这篇文章可能也有一些不准确的地方，我很久以前就测试过其中一些，但我从未真正运行过最后两个。

children.author: grapheneos
children.textRawHtml:
> It doesn't solve the current issue

These operating systems aren't compatible with most of the apps and services people want to use. It's going to become much worse. The compatibility layers several provide have extremely poor compatibility combined with disabling the Android security model and app sandbox. Apps running in those compatibility layers are much less contained with less isolation from the Linux kernel, not more.

Aside from that, many people care about privacy and security. Each of those operating systems is far less private and drastically less secure than the Android Open Source Project. None has a truly complete and working app sandbox or permission model. None uses modern exploit protections. None has serious hardware-based encryption features needed to protect against data extraction. They're not serious alternatives to an iPhone from a privacy and security perspective as an AOSP-based OS on decent hardware can be.

> but in case we don't manage to push back on this

It's a warning that's being added to Google Mobile Services operating systems. It doesn't negatively impact other operating systems based on the Android Open Source Project.

> various actual linux OSes for mobile

Linux doesn't mean GNU/Linux or systemd/Linux. It doesn't at all imply using glibc, systemd, GNU coreutils, Bash, GNOME, etc. Distributions using different userspace components including several of the ones you've listed are still Linux Android-based operating systems including AOSP and GrapheneOS are Linux distributions. Alpine doesn't use glibc and SailfishOS has a lot of their own mix of open and closed source software. Using a typical desktop Linux userspace stack isn't what makes it Linux and there's also not a lot of consistency in what's used on desktops regardless. A Linux distribution not using musl, glibc, GNU coreutils, etc. is still Linux.

> There are many more linux mobile OSes, but as far as I know these are the main ones. There might also be some inaccuracies on this post, I tested some of these a long time ago, and I never actually run the last 2.

AOSP-based mobile operating systems are Linux distributions.
children.textZhHtml:
> 它不能解决当前问题

这些操作系统与人们想要使用的大多数应用程序和服务不兼容。情况会变得更糟。几个提供的兼容性层的兼容性极差，并且禁用了 Android 安全模型和应用程序沙箱。在这些兼容层中运行的应用程序受到的限制要少得多，与 Linux 内核的隔离也更少，而不是更多。

除此之外，许多人都关心隐私和安全。这些操作系统的私密性和安全性都远不如 Android 开源项目。没有一个具有真正完整且有效的应用程序沙箱或权限模型。没有一个使用现代的漏洞保护。没有一个具有严格的基于硬件的加密功能来防止数据提取。从隐私和安全的角度来看，它们并不是 iPhone 的严肃替代品，而在体面的硬件上基于 AOSP 的操作系统则可以做到这一点。

> 但如果我们无法阻止这一点

这是一个正在添加到 Google 移动服务操作系统中的警告。它不会对基于 Android 开源项目的其他操作系统产生负面影响。

>各种实际的移动 Linux 操作系统

Linux 并不意味着 GNU/Linux 或 systemd/Linux。它根本不意味着使用 glibc、systemd、GNU coreutils、Bash、GNOME 等。使用不同用户空间组件（包括您列出的几个组件）的发行版仍然是基于 Linux Android 的操作系统，包括 AOSP 和 GrapheneOS 都是 Linux 发行版。 Alpine 不使用 glibc，而 SailfishOS 有很多自己的开源和闭源软件组合。使用典型的桌面 Linux 用户空间堆栈并不是 Linux 的本质，而且桌面上使用的内容也没有太多一致性。不使用 musl、glibc、GNU coreutils 等的 Linux 发行版仍然是 Linux。

> Linux 移动操作系统还有很多，但据我所知，这些是主要的。这篇文章可能也有一些不准确的地方，我很久以前测试了其中一些，并且我从未真正运行过最后两个。

基于 AOSP 的移动操作系统是 Linux 发行版。

children.author: ipaddr
children.textRawHtml:
"Apps running in those compatibility layers are much less contained with less isolation from the Linux kernel, not more."

Being isolated a little bit more from the kernal offers an illusion of privacy meanwhile where you are, what you have installed, your photos and friends are available to other apps at a much higher level. I understand being able to slow down a nation state actor is important but most privacy concerns for average people happen at the OS level not the kernel.
children.textZhHtml:
“在这些兼容层中运行的应用程序受到的限制要少得多，与 Linux 内核的隔离也更少，而不是更多。”

与内核的隔离程度更高一点会带来一种隐私的错觉，同时您所在的位置、您安装的内容、您的照片和朋友都可以在更高级别上供其他应用程序使用。 我知道能够减慢国家行为者的速度很重要，但普通人的大多数隐私问题都发生在操作系统级别而不是内核。

children.author: HybridStatAnim8
children.textRawHtml: This claim is false. Isolation and protection from the kernel is vital and it is already targeted for exploitation, and will be targeted even more as time goes on. Properly updating the kernel and improving its isolation and security is the bare minimum for even below average users, not just for high threat models. The claims you are making are unsubstantiated.
children.textZhHtml: 这种说法是错误的。内核的隔离和保护至关重要，它已经成为被利用的目标，而且随着时间的推移，被针对的情况会越来越多。正确更新内核并改善其隔离和安全性，即使是对于低于平均水平的用户来说也是最低要求，而不仅仅是针对高威胁模型。你提出的这些说法毫无根据。

children.author: janvlug
children.textRawHtml:
I'm using a Librem 5 as my daily phone. PureOS is actively developed and based on Debian. Monthly development updates are published here: https://puri.sm/posts/tag/advanced-readers/

Personally, I do not use Android apps on the Librem 5, but Waydroid is available in the PureOS repository. Waydroid is a container-based approach to boot a full Android system on regular GNU/Linux systems running Wayland based desktop environments (like PureOS).

PureOS also provides convergence via Phosh. Convergence means here that the same app can be used on a phone and on a big screen, the GUI adjusts to the available screen size.

Phosh aims to provide a daily-usable, robust and easy to use graphical user environment for mobile devices running mainline Linux. Phosh was originally initiated by developers from Purism for the Librem 5 phone but is nowadays used on many different devices covering smartphones, tablets and convertibles. It has even been seen on laptops.
children.textZhHtml:
我使用 Librem 5 作为我的日常手机。 PureOS 正在积极开发并基于 Debian。每月开发更新发布在这里：https://puri.sm/posts/tag/advanced-readers/

就我个人而言，我不在 Librem 5 上使用 Android 应用程序，但 Waydroid 在 PureOS 存储库中可用。 Waydroid 是一种基于容器的方法，可在运行基于 Wayland 的桌面环境（如 PureOS）的常规 GNU/Linux 系统上启动完整的 Android 系统。

PureOS 还通过 Phosh 提供聚合。融合在这里意味着同一个应用程序可以在手机和大屏幕上使用，GUI 会根据可用的屏幕尺寸进行调整。

Phosh 旨在为运行主线 Linux 的移动设备提供日常可用、强大且易于使用的图形用户环境。 Phosh 最初是由 Purism 的开发人员为 Librem 5 手机发起的，但现在已用于许多不同的设备，包括智能手机、平板电脑和敞篷车。它甚至出现在笔记本电脑上。

children.author: grapheneos
children.textRawHtml:
> Waydroid is a container-based approach to boot a full Android system on regular GNU/Linux systems running Wayland based desktop environments (like PureOS).

No, it's only a partially working form of Android with the privacy/security model largely disabled and poor app compatibility. Waydroid is based on an ancient release of Android and disables the SELinux-based privacy/security model. It doesn't contain apps from each other and has far less protection for the Linux kernel from the apps. It has poor app compatibility and isn't a good approach to running Android in another OS. ChromeOS made a proper better Android container not losing the privacy/security model but migrated to using hardware accelerated virtual machines. It makes a lot more sense to use a VM since current era smartphone hardware fully supports it.

> PureOS also provides convergence via Phosh. Convergence means here that the same app can be used on a phone and on a big screen, the GUI adjusts to the available screen size.

Android Open Source Project has a desktop mode. It has a hardware-based virtualization layer for running desktop Linux applications too including GPU acceleration support.

> Phosh aims to provide a daily-usable, robust and easy to use graphical user environment for mobile devices running mainline Linux.

Android runs fine on mainline Linux. It doesn't require special kernels. That's tied to specific hardware rather than Android.

PureOS has far worse privacy and drastically worse security compared to iOS or AOSP. It's bringing the traditional atrocious privacy and security of desktops to mobile. Librem 5 also combines that with extraordinarily insecure hardware missing basic firmware updates and security protections. As a whole, these make it drastically easier to exploit devices. That includes going back to disk encryption which doesn't work for the average user due to them not using a strong passphrase and not protecting against data extraction with physical access unless the device is turned off.
children.textZhHtml:
> Waydroid 是一种基于容器的方法，可在运行基于 Wayland 的桌面环境（如 PureOS）的常规 GNU/Linux 系统上启动完整的 Android 系统。

不，它只是 Android 的部分工作形式，隐私/安全模型在很大程度上被禁用，应用程序兼容性也很差。 Waydroid 基于 Android 的一个古老版本，并禁用基于 SELinux 的隐私/安全模型。它不包含彼此的应用程序，并且对 Linux 内核免受应用程序的保护要少得多。它的应用程序兼容性很差，并且不是在其他操作系统中运行 Android 的好方法。 ChromeOS 制作了一个更好的 Android 容器，没有失去隐私/安全模型，而是迁移到使用硬件加速虚拟机。使用 VM 更有意义，因为当前时代的智能手机硬件完全支持它。

> PureOS 还通过 Phosh 提供聚合。融合在这里意味着同一个应用程序可以在手机和大屏幕上使用，GUI 会根据可用的屏幕尺寸进行调整。

Android 开源项目有桌面模式。它有一个基于硬件的虚拟化层，用于运行桌面 Linux 应用程序，包括 GPU 加速支持。

> Phosh 旨在为运行主线 Linux 的移动设备提供日常可用、强大且易于使用的图形用户环境。

Android 在主线 Linux 上运行良好。它不需要特殊的内核。这与特定硬件而不是 Android 相关。

与 iOS 或 AOSP 相比，PureOS 的隐私性和安全性要差得多。它将传统的台式机的残酷隐私和安全性带到了移动设备上。 Librem 5 还结合了极其不安全的硬件，缺少基本的固件更新和安全保护。总的来说，这些使得它大大更容易利用设备。这包括回到磁盘加密，这对普通用户不起作用，因为他们没有使用强密码，并且除非设备关闭，否则不会通过物理访问来防止数据提取。

These Linux distros need to support and push Android compatibility, so that people can load F-Droid, Aurora, and Obtainium on them and get most of the Android apps they want. The ability to use both Linux and Android apps should satisfy nearly everyone. A strong message of consumer defiance needs to be sent.

children.author: HybridStatAnim8
children.textRawHtml:
The Android family of operating systems and the forks made from the android open source project are all linux distributions, and linux phones.

Using desktop linux phones and trying to force that as a norm would set privacy and security back substantially.

The inverse of what you suggest, which is Android with desktop linux app compatibility, would be a huge step forward, and is already much closer than you might think.

Modern phones have substantially better VM support in the hardware than in previous models, and it is maturing at a very fast rate. We would be able to run linux VMs on Android, paired with desktop mode, with evidence for USB passthrough for an externel GPU in AOSP

There is also evidence that we will be able to put desktop linux app icons on the Android homescreen and using them in an app-like fashion.

This would use the more secure host to run the VM for the less secure OS.
children.textZhHtml:
Android操作系统家族以及从Android开源项目衍生出的分支都是Linux发行版，也是Linux手机。

使用桌面Linux手机并试图将其强推为常态，将大幅倒退隐私和安全。

与你建议的相反，即Android兼容桌面Linux应用，将是一个巨大的进步，而且这已经比你想象的要近得多了。

现代手机在硬件上的虚拟机支持比以前的型号有了实质性的提升，并且正在以非常快的速度成熟。我们将能够在Android上运行Linux虚拟机，配合桌面模式，并且有证据表明AOSP中支持外部GPU的USB直通。

还有证据表明，我们将能够把桌面Linux应用图标放在Android主屏幕上，并像普通应用一样使用它们。

这将利用更安全的宿主机为安全性较低的操作系统运行虚拟机。

children.author: sambuccid
children.textRawHtml:
Personally I think that Android AOSP relies too much on the support from google, that has demonstrated to happily use anti-competitive practices and not be afraid to close and centralise AOSP as much as possible.

In a possible future where google decides to continue any new update of Android as closed source, will the community have enough people stepping in to support the development of AOSP? Will google allow the Play Store to run on AOSP? If not, do we have enough apps distributed outside of the Play Store to compensate?

My point is that for an OS that shares more code with desktop Linux (same apps, same DE, same terminal programs, ...) we can rely on the existing Linux community to maintain and advance the OS, and then adapt it for mobile, which I think takes considerably less resources
children.textZhHtml:
我个人认为 Android AOSP 过于依赖 Google 的支持，Google 已经表现出乐于使用反竞争做法，并且不惧怕尽可能关闭和中心化 AOSP。

在未来 Google 决定继续将 Android 的任何新更新作为闭源的情况下，社区是否会有足够的人介入来支持 AOSP 的开发？谷歌会允许 Play 商店在 AOSP 上运行吗？如果没有，我们是否有足够的应用程序在 Play 商店之外分发来弥补？

我的观点是，对于与桌面 Linux 共享更多代码的操作系统（相同的应用程序、相同的 DE、相同的终端程序......），我们可以依靠现有的 Linux 社区来维护和改进操作系统，然后将其适应移动设备，我认为这需要更少的资源

children.author: Gander5739
children.textRawHtml: The existing "linux phones" are much worse for general use than AOSP (putting aside that AOSP doesn't run on any device by itself and looking at projects like grapheneos and lineageos instead). If Google decided to fully close off Anddoid (which would presumably violate some licenses somewhere, though I'm not really familiar with the situation), then conceivably the existing AOSP would be a better starting point. In any case, people tend to complain that each new Android update brings fewer and fewer significant changes; maybe only security patches and driver updates would be needed.

children.author: baranul
children.textRawHtml:
The problem is that the usage of Android should not be at the expense of users and developers rights to choose, freedom, or privacy. With that line crossed, Android becomes an unattractive option.

With an increase in mobile Linux (e.g. Mobian and others), they can and will get better. Customers and developers choice and freedom are being nullified, because there are few other options. That has to change.
children.textZhHtml:
问题在于，Android 的使用不应以牺牲用户和开发者的选择权、自由权或隐私权为代价。一旦越过这条线，Android 就会成为一个没有吸引力的选择。

随着移动 Linux（例如 Mobian 等）的增加，它们可以而且将会变得更好。客户和开发人员的选择和自由正在被取消，因为几乎没有其他选择。这必须改变。

comments.author: TrackerFF
comments.textRawHtml:
One important part of the story is in the very beginning: The founder’s motivation. To become wealthy.

You see this in the startup world a lot. Founders with 5+ failed startups in different sectors, because said founder picked the fields mainly by doing some market analysis. Not domain expertise.

There’s then a big mismatch between what the founder thinks is possible, and what the domain expert thinks is possible.

The defense is of course that some people can do that - Musk did it, so why not?

Another defense is that blindingly naïve optimism is sometimes needed to move the needle, as the concept “that can’t be done” simply doesn’t exist to some people.

I’ve sat through some pitches like that, where it is very obvious that the founder/CEO has limited knowledge and expertise in what they’re pitching, where the product is limited, but their enthusiasm is off the charts.

EDIT: The very latest happened only a couple of weeks ago. A startup had reached out to my employer as they’re developing a platform in our domain. Higher ups liked what they’d seen, enough to arrange a real meeting.

Startup is only 3 months old, and the moment I opened the platform I recognized a vibecoded (likely using clause) platform identical to almost all other launched on a daily basis.

So I probed a bit about data sources, serious questions regarding security, etc. but the guy was pretty fluent in consultant (turns out he had worked as a management consultant before launching), and the CTO was just nodding along.

In the end they wanted our data, and promised the moon on features - but as mentioned, I’m sure the whole product was entirely vibecoded.
comments.textZhHtml:
故事的一个重要部分就在一开始：创始人的动机。致富。

这种情况在创业界屡见不鲜。创始人在不同行业有超过 5 家失败的初创公司，因为创始人主要通过做一些市场分析来选择领域。不是领域专业知识。

创始人认为可能的事情与领域专家认为可能的事情之间存在很大的不匹配。

辩解当然是有些人可以做到这一点 - 马斯克做到了，那么为什么不呢？

另一种辩解是有时需要令人盲目的天真乐观来推动发展，因为“不可能做到”的概念对某些人来说根本不存在

我参加过一些这样的推介，很明显，创始人/首席执行官对他们所推介的内容的知识和专业知识有限，产品也有限，但他们的热情却很高。

编辑：最新的情况发生在几周前。一家初创公司联系了我的雇主，因为他们正在我们领域开发一个平台。高层很喜欢他们所看到的，足以安排一次真正的会议。

初创公司只有 3 个月大，当我打开平台的那一刻，我认出了一个与几乎所有其他每天推出的平台相同的 vivicoded（可能使用条款）平台。

所以我探讨了一些关于数据源、有关安全的严重问题等。点头同意。

最后他们想要我们的数据，并承诺在功能上满载而归 - 但正如前面提到的，我确信整个产品完全是通过 vivicode 编码的。

children.author: overgard
children.textRawHtml:
I focus on the negatives because they're so massive. Elon didn't even start Tesla, he was an investor that forced the founder out. I'll concede that SpaceX is a little better, but even then a lot of his ideas are profoundly stupid (while I think colonizing the solar system is worthwhile, doing it on Mars is very dumb. You'd need to build underground bunkers so people don't die of cancer from radiation, and there aren't that many useful natural resources there. The moon is much more useful from a practical standpoint of creating easier access to the rest of the solar system, and Titan makes a lot more sense for a sustainable colony. But Elon is a toddler so he just went "Mars!!")

Lets throw in a few more of Elon's failures, this time lets focus on the ones that are outright dangerous or killing people!

- In an accident, the Cybertruck has frequently trapped people inside the vehicle and caused them to burn to death when the batteries catch fire because they hid the manual release on the doors for aesthetic reasons, so people couldn't get out. Also the unbreakable glass means that people spent their final moments desperately trying to smash an unbreakable window while burning to death. People have died from this! It was totally predictable and preventable! https://www.ktvu.com/news/parents-woman-killed-cybertruck-cr...

- Fully self driving. Hasn't happened, and a lot of the danger is because he wanted to cheap out on lidar and brashly overpromised on its capabilities.

Lets also focus on some of the funny ones that came from his need for validation, like needing people to think he's a great gamer: https://www.theguardian.com/games/2025/jan/20/elon-musk-stan... (why would any adult not involved in E-Sports even want to convince their peers they're great at gaming? Like, who cares?!)

Even when he has a good idea like Starlink he ruins it by awful about it (shutting it off for entities he disagrees with).

This guy should not be anyone's hero.

comments.author: digitaltrees
comments.textRawHtml:
I am migrating my company to private open source AI and building custom tooling, orchestration, an IDE and harness around it. The commercial labs have show it’s far too risky to build on top of them.

With the labs moving into the app layer every interaction with the API related to product development or innovation is data they will steal and use to compete against you by adding those features to their everything apps. No one would hire a knowledge firm (law firm, accountants, management consultants) if they could steal your information and give it to your competitors. Their actions have remained me that we can’t tolerate that with AI systems.

With spaceX buying cursor and Google buying windsurf, and gathering all the developer interaction as training data, there is too much risk that development process itself will be rug pulled.
comments.textZhHtml:
我正在将我的公司迁移到私有开源 AI，并围绕它构建自定义工具、编排、IDE 和工具。商业实验室已经表明，在它们之上进行构建风险太大。

随着实验室进入应用程序层，与产品开发或创新相关的 API 的每一次交互都是他们将窃取的数据，并通过将这些功能添加到他们的所有应用程序中来与您竞争。如果知识公司（律师事务所、会计师、管理顾问）能够窃取您的信息并将其提供给您的竞争对手，那么没有人会雇用他们。他们的行为一直让我觉得我们不能容忍人工智能系统出现这种情况。

随着 SpaceX 购买 Cursor 和 Google 购买 Windsurf，并将所有开发人员交互收集为训练数据，开发过程本身被拖垮的风险太大了。

children.author: aspenmartin
children.textRawHtml:
This makes sense but I am not comfortable with the open source picture either. It depends on the use case and long term strategy. If you're handling customer support tickets or something, beyond some capability I can't imagine the ROI would be worth needing the absolute frontier. You are then in a wonderfully lovely place: absolutely full control over the model and inference stack (if you want).

But

- Plenty of businesses are stuck between a rock and a hard place: make 3p models load bearing, or sacrifice performance to the competitors that are willing to swallow that risk

- I just cannot imagine a viable equilibrium where OSS models compete on capabilities with the frontier without a hidden payer and shaky economics. Quant funds, some sovereign AI effort, cloud business, sanctioned distilled frontier models; all of these are demonstrably viable vehicles for OSS development. But the optimal position for these purposes is not to be the best, it's to be good enough (which I think is the position they find themselves in). I can imagine temporary points where open models pull ahead but not sustainably.

I agree there are many many use cases where the optimal choice is to reach for open weight models (I assume yours is one of them).

But the economics of frontier model development necessitates that these models are behind and thats a problem for other cases.
children.textZhHtml:
这是有道理的，但我对开源图片也不满意。这取决于用例和长期策略。如果您正在处理客户支持票证或其他东西，超出某些能力，我无法想象投资回报率是否值得需要绝对前沿。然后你就处于一个非常可爱的地方：完全控制模型和推理堆栈（如果你愿意的话）。

但是

-很多企业陷入进退两难的境地：让 3p 模型承载，或者牺牲性能给愿意承担这种风险的竞争对手

-我无法想象一种可行的平衡，在这种平衡中，OSS 模型在没有隐藏支付者和不稳定经济的情况下与前沿技术进行能力竞争。量化基金、一些主权人工智能项目、云业务、经认可的前沿模型；所有这些都是开放源码软件开发的明显可行的工具。但实现这些目的的最佳位置不是最好，而是足够好（我认为这就是他们所处的位置）。我可以想象开放模型领先但不可持续的临时点。

我同意在很多用例中，最佳选择是采用开放权重模型（我假设你的模型就是其中之一）。

但是前沿模型开发的经济学要求这些模型落后，这对于其他情况来说是一个问题。

children.author: digitaltrees
children.textRawHtml:
I am not sure I understand your arguments. You seem to be taking both positions. I suspect that is because there are a few confounding variables and diverse needs AI solves and that makes a cohesive analysis hard to reach. I have a clear view that cuts through the complexity, whether you find it useful or persuasive is another matter but I’ll lay it out.

1. All intelligence has value. A Harvard MBA or Stanford CS engineer have value. Haiku has value, Opus has value.

2. Sometimes it makes sense to buy, build or rent capabilities. But lack of ownership of something your business requires to operate is an existential risk which requires ownership.

3. Current OSS models are good enough for substantial automation and productivity gains with the right orchestration and harness. Especially with domain experts in the loop.

4. Frontier models while better, introduce existential risk; so they are a temptation that should be ignored if you care about your business being able to continue to exist.

Let’s invert this conclusion. Imagine you build a workflow around Fable 5 and the workflow going down would harm your business. Well the government has demonstrated ability and willingness to impose a multi day / week / month interruption in your business with no legally required notice, right to appeal or compensation. And further demonstrates a willingness to give preferential access to your competitors.

For these reasons, the only viable path is a model you have on servers you control and can replace if needed. That means cloud providers are fine if you have the model archived somewhere.
children.textZhHtml:
我不确定我是否理解您的论点。你似乎同时担任这两个职位。我怀疑这是因为人工智能解决了一些令人困惑的变量和多样化的需求这使得很难进行有凝聚力的分析。我有一个清晰的观点，可以消除复杂性，你是否觉得它有用或有说服力是另一回事，但我会列出来。

1.所有的智力都有价值。哈佛 MBA 或斯坦福计算机科学工程师很有价值。俳句有价值，作品有价值。

2.有时购买、构建或租赁功能是有意义的。但是，缺乏对企业运营所需的东西的所有权是一种存在风险，需要所有权。

3.当前的 OSS 模型足以通过正确的编排和利用来大幅提高自动化和生产力。尤其是领域专家参与其中。

4。前沿模型虽然更好，但引入了存在风险；因此，如果您关心自己的企业能否继续存在，那么就应该忽略它们的诱惑。

让我们反转这个结论。想象一下，您围绕《神鬼寓言 5》构建了一个工作流程，而该工作流程的崩溃会损害您的业务。政府已经表现出了能力和意愿，可以在没有法律要求的通知、上诉权或赔偿的情况下对您的业务实施多天/周/月的中断。并进一步表明愿意为竞争对手提供优先访问权限。

出于这些原因，唯一可行的途径是您在自己控制的服务器上拥有一个模型，并且可以在需要时进行替换。这意味着如果您将模型存档在某个地方，云提供商就可以了。

children.author: aspenmartin
children.textRawHtml:
I agree with everything you're saying. And I would also agree if you can run your business adequately on OSS models you should absolutely do that for exactly the reasons you say. All I mean is: frontier models will always have their place and I don't see a way for OSS to ever change that picture. I also worry that the economics that makes meaningful open weights model development possible may break someday but I don't really see a mechanism for this at this point.

I don't actually have a use case in mind where you would _need_ or get a substantial competitive advantage of the absolute frontier in some sort of workflow or whatever load bearing DAG there is for your business. But if there are cases like this, that will suck.
children.textZhHtml:
我同意你所说的一切。我也同意，如果您可以在 OSS 模型上充分运行您的业务，那么您绝对应该这样做，原因正是您所说的。我的意思是：前沿模型将永远占有一席之地，而且我认为 OSS 无法改变这种情况。我还担心，使有意义的开放权重模型开发成为可能的经济学可能有一天会被打破，但目前我还没有真正看到这种机制。

我实际上并没有想到一个用例，您需要或在某种工作流程或任何适合您的业务的承载 DAG 中获得绝对前沿的实质性竞争优势。但如果有有这样的情况，那就糟糕了。

children.textRawHtml:
Red hat did it for Linux. Someone needs to do the same for AI. Make money on the implementation and maintenance not the api tokens. Tech companies have gotten fat and Happy on recurring revenue. But they are abusing the trust users have placed in them. I feel a back lash in a way I’ve never experienced.

I think AI labs are assuming that if they build and serve intelligence every one will want it. I think the last month has resulted in a lot of people saying “f u I would rather fail in life than serve you”. I personally went from feeling excited and optimistic about building amazing things on top of the models to hell bent on being self reliant and lots of my friends are doing the same.

我认为AI实验室假设，只要他们构建并提供智能，每个人都会想要它。我认为过去一个月让很多人开始说“去你的，我宁愿人生失败也不愿为你服务”。我个人从最初对在模型之上构建出色产品感到兴奋和乐观，转变为铁了心要自力更生，我的许多朋友也是如此。

> I think the last month has resulted in a lot of people saying “f u I would rather fail in life than serve you”.

I agree a lot of people are saying this and doing this but this won't last long. People do not want to suffer or fall behind their peers. children.textZhHtml:

children.author: digitaltrees
children.textRawHtml:
The thing the overlords are forgetting is they need customers and users.

You are right that the temptation to use the asymmetric advantage AI gives will be intense. But banks felt that temptation in 2008 in with cheap capital and 40x leverage. Some banks resisted. Which ones do you think survived in 2010? Business and technology moves in cycles. Optimize for longevity and let your competitors go out of business
children.textZhHtml:

comments.author: nlh
comments.textRawHtml:
Here's a copy of the letter that Commerce sent to Anthropic (note who it'a NOT addressed to...)

Source: https://x.com/AndrewCurran_/status/2072103733715194048?s=20

-------

June 30, 2026

Tom Brown Chief Compute Officer Anthropic 548 Market Street San Francisco, CA 94104

Dear Mr. Brown:

Since the issuance of my previous letters, dated June 12, 2026 and June 26, 2026, Anthropic has taken steps in close coordination with the U.S. government to address the risks associated with Claude Mythos 5 and Claude Fable 5. Among other things, Anthropic has agreed to proactively detect and address security risks associated with the models; to work diligently with the U.S. government on protocols and standards and releases for Mythos, Fable, and future models; and to inform the U.S. government of any malicious activity.

In light of these actions and commitments, as well as the Bureau of Industry and Security's evaluation of the diversion risks now presented by Claude Mythos 5 and Claude Fable 5, the controls in the June 12 letter are withdrawn. A license is no longer required for the export, reexport, or in-country transfer, including deemed export or deemed reexport, of the Mythos or Fable models.

Commerce reserves the right to reevaluate the decisions made in this letter and the necessity of reimposing a license requirement, should circumstances change or should Anthropic fail to adhere to its commitments.

If you have any questions about this letter, please contact me or the Under Secretary of Commerce for Industry and Security, Jeffrey Kessler, at (202) 255-1864.

Sincerely,

Howard W. Lutnick

------

comments.author: linzhangrun
comments.textRawHtml:
For pure interaction, CarPlay as a generic solution is very hard to beat infotainment systems that are deeply integrated with the vehicle itself. Its advantages are mainly these:

- Consistency. You get into a new car, all you need to figure out is how to open CarPlay, no need to learning a completely different and often complicated infotainment system.

- "It's on your phone". You can decide what playlist to play or where to navigate before you even get into the car.

- Stays up to date over the long term. Just look at cars from five years ago. Most built-in infotainment systems are still stuck in that era, no matter how smart they once looked. CarPlay uses your phone as the main computing platform, and the car's infotainment system only needs to act as a thin client for I/O, it keeps updating with iOS.
comments.textZhHtml:
就纯粹的交互体验而言，CarPlay作为一种通用解决方案，很难被与车辆深度集成的车载信息娱乐系统所超越。它的优势主要体现在以下几个方面：

- 一致性。当你坐进一辆新车时，只需弄清楚如何打开CarPlay即可，无需去学习一套完全不同且往往复杂的车载信息娱乐系统。

- “一切都在手机上”。你甚至在上车前就能决定播放什么歌单或导航去哪里。

- 长期保持更新。看看五年前的车就知道了。大多数内置信息娱乐系统至今仍停留在过去的水平，无论它们曾经看起来多么智能。CarPlay将你的手机作为主要计算平台，车载信息娱乐系统只需充当输入输出的瘦客户端，并随着iOS不断更新。

children.author: rkangel
children.textRawHtml:
The big thing for me is that the lifetime of a car is a lot longer than the technology development lifecycle.

I just bought a 10 year old Toyota estate (station wagon). It's got a reasonable screen and Bluetooth implementation etc. But I'm never going to want to use the built in navigation because it's just not as good as what my phone will do. And the audio integration isn't as sophisticated as it might be - I have to choose the app on my phone.

Whereas CarPlay/AndroidAuto is generic from the car point of view, and as phone features and software improve your car capability evolves too.
children.textZhHtml:
对我来说最重要的一点是，汽车的使用寿命远远长于技术的发展周期。

我刚买了一辆10年车龄的丰田旅行车。它配备了不错的屏幕和蓝牙功能等等。但我绝不会想用它的内置导航，因为它根本比不上手机导航。而且音频集成也不够完善——我必须在手机上选择应用。

而从汽车的角度来看，CarPlay/Android Auto是通用的，随着手机功能和软件的升级，你的车载体验也会随之进化。

children.author: titanomachy
children.textRawHtml: I’m sure the car manufacturers would much prefer if you updated your car as often as you do your phone.
children.textZhHtml: 我敢肯定，汽车制造商更希望你换车的频率能像换手机一样高。

children.author: bluGill
children.textRawHtml:
They want everyone to, but they also know that the cost of cars, it's not realistic for that to happen. The only way even people on the upper middle class can afford to be buying or leasing cars every three years is because at the end of those three years the car still has a lot of value left to sell on to someone who is more middle class. And that middle class person will likely sell it again to someone who is a little bit lower down and at about 10 to 12 years it finally reaches someone who is poor.

The only way someone could buy a car more often is if they became a lot cheaper. That would mean doing away with a lot of the luxury parts of the car that are where the profit is.
children.textZhHtml:
他们希望所有人都能这么做，但他们也知道，以现在的汽车成本，这并不现实。即使是中上层阶级的人，之所以能负担得起每三年买一次或租一次车，唯一的原因就是三年期满后，这辆车还有很大的剩余价值，可以卖给更普通的中产阶级。而这个中产阶级的人很可能又会把它卖给收入再低一点的人，到了大约10到12年车龄时，它最终会落到穷人手里。

人们想要更频繁地换车，唯一的办法就是让车价大幅降低。这就意味着要取消汽车上很多用来赚取利润的豪华配置。

children.author: xp84
children.textRawHtml: They’re working on it. I follow a mechanic on YouTube who has shared dozens of examples of manufacturers seemingly intentionally shipping deeply flawed components for seemingly no reason other than to push the average lifespan of a vehicle back downwards from the high levels we’d reached by about 2012. Cars are being junked with less than 100k miles because the original parts fail, replacement parts are hard to get, and now manufacturers are making it so you need an expensive piece of software which they completely control to program a new part to the car, without which, it’s a brick. So essentially they can drop support for a car from that software, and make it near-impossible to replace those parts even if someone reverse-engineers aftermarket parts.
children.textZhHtml: 他们正在这么做。我在YouTube上关注了一位修车师傅，他分享了数十个例子，说明汽车制造商似乎在故意发运存在严重缺陷的零部件，似乎毫无其他原因，只是为了把车辆的平均使用寿命从我们在2012年左右达到的高水平重新拉低。现在有些车行驶里程还不到10万英里就被报废了，因为原厂件坏了，替换件又很难弄到，而且现在制造商还搞出这么一套：你需要用一款完全由他们控制的昂贵软件来给新车编程，否则这车就是一块砖头。所以本质上，他们可以从那款软件中取消对某款车的支持，让那些零件几乎无法更换，即使有人逆向工程搞出了副厂件也没用。

children.author: SoftTalker
children.textRawHtml:
This isn't new. Automakers have always wanted to build to price and to survive the warranty period. Only competition works against it. Japanese makers competed on affordability and reliability from the 1970s through the 2010s or so, but now they've adopted the same compromises and are skating on their reputations. Honda for example is no longer affordable, nor as reliable or well made as they used to be, though everyone still thinks they are. Even Mercedes, once the king of overbuilt cars, are now just overpriced tin and plastic like any other car.

Hopefully some makers will start competing on affordability and maintainability again. Slate might be one.
children.textZhHtml:
这并非什么新鲜事。汽车制造商一直希望卡着成本造车，只求熬过保修期就行。只有竞争才能打破这种局面。从20世纪70年代到2010年左右，日本车企凭借经济性和可靠性展开竞争，但现在他们也采取了同样的妥协策略，全靠透支品牌声誉吃老本。以本田为例，现在的车不再便宜，也不像过去那样可靠和做工精良，尽管大家仍抱有这种刻板印象。就连曾经以用料扎实著称的奔驰，现在也和其他车一样，沦为用铁皮和塑料堆砌的高价货。

希望一些车企能重新在经济性和易维护性上展开竞争。Slate或许就是其中之一。

children.author: linzhangrun
children.textRawHtml: Current State of Chinese EV Manufacturers
children.textZhHtml: 中国电动汽车制造商现状

children.author: xp84
children.textRawHtml: Source? They’re cheaper, but I’ve seen zero proof they are less long-lasting than our cars. If anything, those who have tried them say they’re better cars, including the Ford CEO, who imported one and can’t say enough good things about it.
children.textZhHtml: 来源呢？它们确实更便宜，但我完全没看到任何证据表明它们不如我们的车耐用。相反，试过的人都说它们的车更好，就连福特CEO也进口了一辆，并且对其赞不绝口。

children.author: linzhangrun
children.textRawHtml:
Believe me, I am a Chinese who follows the Chinese car market quite closely.

Since the endless price war started in 2023, many cars in the tens-of-thousands to low-hundreds-of-thousands RMB range (affordable by most people) have had serious quality problems because of price cuts, including joint-venture cars (foreign-brand cars produced in Chinese factories).

This is the world’s largest car market, where the best-selling ICE car last month could only rank 17th overall. It is also a market where you can buy an Accord for under $20,000, and a Corolla for a little over $11,000. And both are going to be discontinued because it is no longer competitive and sells badly.

When the price goes up, the car itself is usually much better. At least it is not like earlier cheap BYDs, which could start rusting everywhere after just one year.

But the very short development and production cycle is another big problem. In this fiercely competitive market, sometimes it takes less than half a year from the birth of a car brand to a model launch. Recent examples are Huawei GT7.

Because new cars are getting better so quickly, many cars cannot keep sales for more than six months. Some are discontinued in less than a year, like Han L and Tang L. Some cars even get three updates in one year, like Zeekr 001, just to keep up with new cars.

This obviously creates maintenance and after-sales problems.

By comparison, Xiaomi, which changes generation every two years, is already one of the most traditional and conservative ones. This is also the car Ford’s CEO “imported one and can’t say enough good things about it”. I have driven it too, haha.
children.textZhHtml:
相信我，我是一个密切关注中国汽车市场的中国人。

自从2023年无休止的价格战打响以来，许多售价在几万到十几万元人民币区间（大多数人都能负担得起）的车型，因为降价而出现了严重的质量问题，其中也包括合资车（在中国工厂生产的外国品牌汽车）。

这是全球最大的汽车市场，上个月最畅销的燃油车在总榜上只能排到第17位。这也是一个你能以不到2万美元买到雅阁，以1.1万多美元买到卡罗拉的市场。而这两款车都将停产，因为它们已不再具备竞争力，销量惨淡。

当价格上去后，车本身的品质通常也会好很多。至少不会像早期那些廉价的比亚迪，开了一年就到处生锈。

但极短的研发和生产周期是另一个大问题。在这个竞争极其激烈的市场里，有时从一个汽车品牌的诞生到车型上市，用不了半年时间。最近的例子就是华为GT7。

因为新车进步太快，很多车的销量热度维持不了六个月。有些车上市不到一年就停产了，比如汉L和唐L。有些车甚至一年改款三次，比如极氪001，仅仅是为了跟上新一代车型的步伐。

这显然带来了维修和售后问题。

相比之下，两年才换代一次的小米，已经算是其中最传统、最保守的了。这也是福特CEO“自己进口了一辆，赞不绝口”的那款车。我也开过，哈哈。

children.author: HPsquared

children.author: doxeddaily
children.textRawHtml:
This is a good point. It's a form of future proofing. Hopefully Apple doesn't drop the ball too hard on backward compatibility.

For me I just want a set of consistent things from car to car. I have a few different vehicles I might switch between on any given day. Carplay works in all of them.
children.textZhHtml:

对我来说，我只希望在不同车辆间切换时能有一致的体验。我平时可能会在几辆不同的车之间换着开，而CarPlay在所有这些车里都能完美运行。

stories.title: The bottleneck might be the air in the room
stories.titleZh: 瓶颈可能在于室内空气。

comments.author: gpt5
comments.textRawHtml:
I really wish a Apple or another major OEM would integrate CO2 monitor into watches or smartphones. Suddenly, everybody would be aware of the CO2 level in the room, get alerts, etc. and the problem will just solve itself.

There are so many rooms, classrooms, movie theaters and other places with poor ventilation where you just feel dizzy, or fall asleep, not knowing it was just due to lower oxygen levels in your blood. Raising awareness is the only real solution.
comments.textZhHtml:
我真的希望苹果或其他主要 OEM 将二氧化碳监测器集成到手表或智能手机中。突然之间，每个人都会意识到房间内的二氧化碳浓度，收到警报等，问题就会自行解决。

有很多房间、教室、电影院和其他通风不良的地方，您只是感到头晕或睡着，却不知道这只是由于血液中的氧气含量较低所致。提高认识是唯一真正的解决方案。

children.author: Aurornis
children.textRawHtml:
> I really wish a Apple or another major OEM would integrate CO2 monitor into watches or smartphones

CO2 levels are locally elevated in the area where you exhale. Someone sitting at a desk with their hands on a keyboard exhaling through their nose would be producing a directed stream of elevated CO2 straight at the sensor on their wrist. Same thing if someone puts their phone on their desk.

Even with the IKEA and other cheap sensors that are becoming popular, there is a learning curve where users discover that putting it on their desk right in front of where they’re breathing produces higher numbers than having it even 5 feet away.

The false positives from having a CO2 sensor that close to everyone’s face would be causing unnecessary alarm all over.

> There are so many rooms, classrooms, movie theaters and other places with poor ventilation where you just feel dizzy, or fall asleep, not knowing it was just due to lower oxygen levels in your blood. Raising awareness is the only real solution.

If someone is falling asleep in this many different places I would suspect undiagnosed sleep apnea or another condition first and foremost. Spaces like movie theaters have very high volumes of air due to their size and commercial building HVAC has much higher standards for air circulation than even your home. If someone was falling asleep in so many different places then the most likely common theme is that person and it should be checked out!

This is another reason why putting CO2 sensors on everyone’s wrists would be a mistake: It would start getting blamed for every vague condition people experience. This has already happened with wrist-worn heart rate monitors. My friends in the medical field see people all the time who come in with vague complaints and they’ve self diagnosed as being related to their heart because they can see their heart rate now.

You also have the wrong idea about what elevated CO2 does. It doesn’t reduce the oxygen levels in your blood. It makes it more difficult for your body to expel CO2, which can produce subtle changes in many processes.
children.textZhHtml:
> 我真的希望苹果或其他主要 OEM 将二氧化碳监测器集成到手表或智能手机中

您呼气区域的二氧化碳水平局部升高。坐在办公桌前，双手放在键盘上，通过鼻子呼气的人会直接在手腕上的传感器上产生一股升高的二氧化碳流。如果有人把手机放在桌子上，情况也是一样。

即使使用宜家和其他越来越流行的廉价传感器，用户也会发现，将其放在桌子上他们呼吸的地方正前方产生的数字比放置在 5 英尺远的地方产生的数字更高。

将 CO2 传感器靠近每个人的脸会产生误报，从而引起不必要的警报。

> 房间、教室、电影院太多了。以及其他通风不良的地方，您会感到头晕或睡着，却不知道这只是由于血液中的氧气含量较低所致。提高认识是唯一真正的解决方案。

如果有人在这么多不同的地方入睡，我首先会怀疑患有未确诊的睡眠呼吸暂停或其他疾病。电影院等空间由于其规模而具有非常高的空气量，而商业建筑的暖通空调对空气流通的标准甚至比您的家还要高得多。如果有人在这么多不同的地方睡着了，那么最有可能的共同主题就是那个人，应该检查一下！

这是为什么将二氧化碳传感器戴在每个人的手腕上会是一个错误的另一个原因：它会开始因人们经历的每一个模糊状况而受到指责。腕戴式心率监测器已经发生过这种情况。我在医学领域的朋友经常看到有人带着模糊的抱怨而来，他们自我诊断为与心脏有关，因为他们现在可以看到自己的心率。

您对二氧化碳升高的作用也有错误的认识。它不会降低血液中的氧气含量。它使您的身体更难排出二氧化碳，从而在许多过程中产生微妙的变化。

children.author: riquito
children.textRawHtml:
> CO2 levels are locally elevated in the area where you exhale.

That's the same area we inhale from... Wouldn't be right to measure there then? It's not like we're interested in the amount of CO2 on the ground (in this discussion)
children.textZhHtml:
> 您呼气的区域的二氧化碳水平局部升高。

这与我们吸气的区域相同...那么在那里测量不是正确的吗？我们对地面上的二氧化碳含量并不感兴趣（在本次讨论中）

children.author: Aurornis
children.textRawHtml:
No, because the studies that establish the levels correlated with cognitive changes use ambient CO2 levels. Not with a stream of air directed, maybe, from your nose to your wrist.

It is not possible to come up with a different baseline for wrist-worn monitors because the measurements could change significantly based on even small factors like the position of the wrist or smartphone.
children.textZhHtml:
不，因为那些确定与认知变化相关的二氧化碳浓度的研究，使用的是环境二氧化碳浓度。而不是可能从你鼻子直接吹到手腕上的一股气流。

无法为腕戴式监测仪设定不同的基准值，因为即使是手腕或智能手机位置的微小变化，都可能导致测量结果发生显著改变。

children.author: hansvm
children.textRawHtml:
That makes sense to me. There's a causal link between ambient air CO2 levels and cognitive changes, but the air in front of your face, which you breathe, doesn't play a role...

A lack of direct study might make "in front of your face" numbers harder to interpret in absolutes, but relatively speaking I do think that's the number which matters more. Whole-room analysis is just a proxy for the air in front of your face.
children.textZhHtml:
这对我来说很合理。环境空气中的二氧化碳浓度与认知变化之间存在因果关系，但你呼吸的脸部前方的空气并没有发挥作用……

缺乏直接研究可能会让“脸部前方”的数值在绝对意义上更难解读，但相对而言，我认为这个数值才更重要。整个房间的分析只是脸部前方空气的一个替代指标。

children.author: popalchemist
children.textRawHtml: Presumably after a month or so of being worn during the day and taken off at night (when the breathing doesn't apply -- and the device knows this), the device could deduce what the influence of the wearer is.
children.textZhHtml: 可以推测，在白天佩戴、晚上摘下（此时呼吸因素不适用——设备也知道这一点）大约一个月后，设备就能推断出佩戴者自身对读数的影响了。

children.author: jason1cho
children.textRawHtml:
Exactly!

I installed a CO2 monitor at home, which reminds me of ventilation in winter when I keep windows closed and heating on.
children.textZhHtml:
完全正确！

我在家里装了一个二氧化碳监测仪，冬天关窗开暖气的时候，它会提醒我通风。

children.author: tzs
children.textRawHtml: Wouldn’t it still be useful because you could look at changes in the measurement? E.g. if I can’t draw any conclusions when my phone says 800 at the start of a meeting because it might be where I’m breathing, but an hour later in the same place it says 2500 couldn’t I conclude we need to take a break and let the room air out?
children.textZhHtml: 它不是仍然有用吗，因为您可以查看测量的变化？例如。如果当我的手机在会议开始时显示 800 时我无法得出任何结论，因为这可能是我呼吸的地方，但一小时后在同一个地方显示 2500，我难道不能得出结论，我们需要休息一下，让房间里的空气流通一下吗？

children.author: cotwo
children.textRawHtml: The air infront of you IS the air you breed in, why shouldn’t it be measured?
children.textZhHtml: 你面前的空气就是你繁殖的空气，为什么不应该测量它？

children.author: Aurornis
children.textRawHtml:
Because the thresholds everyone gets from the studies were not measured in that location.

It would be like the weather station telling you it was 160 degrees outside because they put their thermal sensor on the asphalt, but you wanted to know the air temperature.
children.textZhHtml:
因为大家从研究中得出的阈值并不是在那个位置测量的。

这就好比气象站告诉你室外有160度，因为他们把温度传感器放在了柏油路面上，而你想知道的其实是气温。

children.author: BobaFloutist
children.textRawHtml: Or your watch telling you the room is 98 degrees because it's only ever measuring your body temperature
children.textZhHtml: 或者你的手表告诉你室温是98度，因为它实际上只是在测量你的体温

children.author: Aurornis
children.textRawHtml: That’s a much better example. Thanks
children.textZhHtml: 这个例子好多了。谢谢

children.author: mito88
children.textZhHtml: 98华氏度 = 36.6摄氏度

children.author: Melatonic
children.textRawHtml:
Mostly agreed but with that much data couldn't we easily have an adjusted CO2 number for local sensors ?

I would imagine it's still relative unlike temperature on the wrist (which is too affected by body temp)
children.textZhHtml:
大多数人都同意，但有了这么多数据，我们难道不能轻松地为本地传感器调整 CO2 值吗？

我想它仍然是相对的，与手腕上的温度不同（手腕上的温度太受体温影响）

children.author: avazhi
children.textRawHtml: CO2 does not stream out when you exhale like a fluid. It’s a gas. It dissipates quite immediately and behaves as all gases do - it expands to fill its container via something like Brownian Motion.
children.textZhHtml: 当您呼气时，二氧化碳不会像液体一样流出。它是一种气体。它很快就会消散，并且表现得像所有气体一样 - 它通过布朗运动之类的东西膨胀以填充其容器。

children.author: Aurornis
children.textRawHtml:
Dissipation does not happen instantly.

I’ve done development on products with CO2 sensors and I’ve spent a lot of time with them on my desk right in front of me and also off to the side. Readings right in front of me are predictably higher.

You can breathe into a CO2 sensor 18 inches from your mouth and watch the values spike upward.
children.textZhHtml:
二氧化碳的消散并不是瞬间完成的。

我开发过带二氧化碳传感器的产品，花了很多时间把它们放在办公桌正前方和侧面。正前方的读数不出意料地更高。

你可以对着距离嘴巴18英寸的二氧化碳传感器呼气，看着数值飙升。

children.author: avazhi
children.textRawHtml:
I generally have fans around, but I too use a CO2 detector in my home and have tested it in various places and situations. CO2 concentrations are not as localised as you are making them out to be. If they are elevated in front of my face, for example, I can also similar numbers just above and slightly behind my head. If you go outside and do it, you’ll be surprised that the number doesn’t move really change at all when at arms’ length. Airflow is really everything here.

And, spoiler alert: if the entire area in front of you has an increased CO2 concentration, then your environment has an increased CO2 concentration. That’s the entire point.

Suffice to say I disagree strongly with both the argument that this would lead to hugely erroneous readings and also with the notion that people would panic.
children.textZhHtml:
我平时周围都会开着风扇，但我自己家里也用了二氧化碳检测仪，并在各种地方和情况下都测试过。二氧化碳浓度并没有你说的那么局部化。比如，如果我脸前的浓度升高，我头顶上方和头后方的读数也差不多。如果你到室外去测，你会惊讶地发现，在一臂距离内，数值其实根本没什么变化。在这里，气流才是关键。

而且，提前说一句：如果你面前的整个区域二氧化碳浓度都升高了，那就说明你所在环境的二氧化碳浓度确实升高了。这才是重点。

简而言之，我强烈反对两种观点：一是这会导致读数出现巨大误差，二是人们会因此恐慌。

children.author: Aurornis
children.textRawHtml:
> And, spoiler alert: if the entire area in front of you has an increased CO2 concentration, then your environment has an increased CO2 concentration. That’s the entire point.

I don’t think you’re reading the discussion: The CO2 thresholds that people use are based upon ambient air.

The closer the measurement to your mouth, the higher the reading will be. It should be apparent why putting the sensor directly in front of your mouth will register higher numbers than having it across the room.

CO2 does not instantly diffuse throughout a space. If it did, it would be sufficient to have only very small air leakage in a space for the CO2 to diffuse out of it very rapidly
children.textZhHtml:
> 而且，提前说一句：如果你面前的整个区域二氧化碳浓度都升高了，那就说明你所在环境的二氧化碳浓度确实升高了。这才是重点。

我觉得你没看懂讨论的内容：人们使用的二氧化碳阈值是基于环境空气的。

测量点离你的嘴越近，读数就会越高。把传感器直接放在嘴前测出的数值肯定比放在房间另一头要高，这道理应该很明显。

二氧化碳不会瞬间扩散到整个空间。如果会的话，空间里只要有非常小的空气泄漏，二氧化碳就会非常迅速地扩散出去

children.author: fc417fc802
children.textRawHtml:
Actually what you exhale is a fluid.

The CO2 content is a single chemical species within a mixed gas. Any air currents will cause mixing. Otherwise it undergoes diffusion which is actually a fairly slow process, although much faster in gases than in liquids.
children.textZhHtml:
实际上，你呼出的是流体。

二氧化碳只是混合气体中的一种单一化学物质。任何气流都会导致混合。否则它就会发生扩散，这其实是一个相当缓慢的过程，尽管在气体中比在液体中快得多。

children.author: throw0101a
children.textRawHtml:
> There are so many rooms, classrooms, movie theaters and other places with poor ventilation where you just feel dizzy, or fall asleep, not knowing it was just due to lower oxygen levels in your blood. Raising awareness is the only real solution.

Not wrong, but it is perhaps worth noting that there are already standards for proper ventilation. Generally you're looking at 5–10 cfm/person (2.5-5 L/s), depending on the facility and purpose of the room; see Table 6.2.2.1 in ASHRAE Standard 62.1 for the US:

* https://www.ashrae.org/file%20library/technical%20resources/...

Maybe set up a monitor, but if the room/facility has recently been renovated and meets modern (>2013) building codes, this 'should' have already been taken into account.
children.textZhHtml:
> 有很多房间、教室、电影院和其他通风不良的地方，您会感到头晕或睡着，却不知道这只是由于血液中的氧气含量较低所致。提高认识是唯一真正的解决方案。

这没有错，但也许值得注意的是，已经有了适当通风的标准。一般来说，您需要的流量为 5–10 cfm/人（2.5-5 L/s），具体取决于房间的设施和用途；请参阅美国 ASHRAE 标准 62.1 中的表 6.2.2.1：

* https://www.ashrae.org/file%20library/technical%20resources/...

也许可以设置监视器，但如果房间/设施最近经过翻新并符合现代 (>2013) 建筑规范，则此“应该”已被考虑在内。

stories.title: Why Switzerland has 25 gbit internet and America doesn't
stories.titleZh: 为什么瑞士拥有25 Gbit的互联网，而美国却没有？

comments.author: Aurornis
comments.textRawHtml:
This article came up before. It's heavy on the clickbait, if you couldn't guess from the title.

Some important points that they leave out:

- 25G internet isn't available everywhere in Switzerland. It's just the fastest tier available in some locations.

- The United States is 85 times larger than Switzerland. The entire country of Switzerland is the size of a small US state. Covering the US with broadband is much harder than Switzerland.

- 25G internet is also available in some locations in the United States.

- As another commenter discovered, the average speed test results of US and Swiss internet connections are pretty similar. The average Swiss person isn't connected to the internet faster than the average United States person.
comments.textZhHtml:
这篇文章之前出现过。如果您无法从标题中猜出的话，它的标题诱饵就很重。

他们遗漏了一些重要的观点：

- 25G 互联网并非在瑞士所有地方都可用。这只是某些地区可用的最快等级。

- 美国面积是瑞士的 85 倍。瑞士整个国家的面积相当于美国一个小州的面积。用宽带覆盖美国比瑞士要困难得多。

- 美国部分地区也可以使用 25G 互联网。

- 正如另一位评论者发现的那样，美国和瑞士互联网连接的平均速度测试结果非常相似。瑞士人的平均上网速度并不比美国人的平均速度快。

children.author: Turskarama
children.textRawHtml:
> - The United States is 85 times larger than Switzerland. The entire country of Switzerland is the size of a small US state. Covering the US with broadband is much harder than Switzerland.

I see this argument come up a lot with regards to all kinds of infrastructure, and the thing is it simply _isn't true_.

What matters is population density, GDP per capita, geography, and will. A countries size doesn't matter since twice the area will, all things being equal, also give you twice the workforce to make it happen. In fact the only change a larger area typically makes is better ability to make use of economies of scale, which makes things _easier_.

The only correlation between larger countries and trouble with infrastructure is that a large country is more likely to have large areas with nearly nobody in them, but these areas also typically account for a vanishingly small percentage of the population so they don't really count when people are talking about bad infrastructure.
children.textZhHtml:
> - 美国面积是瑞士的 85 倍。瑞士整个国家的面积相当于美国一个小州的面积。用宽带覆盖美国比瑞士要困难得多。

我看到这种论点在各种基础设施方面经常出现，但事实是这根本_不正确_。

重要的是人口密度、人均 GDP、地理位置和意愿。国家大小并不重要，因为在所有条件相同的情况下，国家面积的两倍也会为你提供两倍的劳动力来实现这一目标。事实上，更大的区域通常带来的唯一变化是更好地利用规模经济，这使得事情变得_更容易_。

大国与基础设施问题之间的唯一相关性是，一个大国更有可能拥有几乎无人居住的大片地区，但这些地区通常只占人口的极小比例，因此当人们谈论糟糕的基础设施时，它们实际上并不算数。

children.author: AnthonyMouse
children.textRawHtml: Population density is the thing people are talking about when they say that. It's 6 times higher in Switzerland than in the US.
children.textZhHtml: 人口密度是人们说这句话时所谈论的事情。瑞士的这一数字是美国的 6 倍。

children.author: SkiFire13
children.textRawHtml: Density is not the correct measure here because empty land decreases density but doesn't need to be covered. What matters is how much the population is clustered.
children.textZhHtml: 密度在这里不是正确的衡量标准，因为空地会降低密度，但不需要覆盖。重要的是人口的聚集程度。

children.author: AnthonyMouse
children.textRawHtml: But that makes it even worse because a lot of Switzerland is entirely unoccupied mountains whereas millions of people live in places like Kansas and Nebraska and expect to have internet access.
children.textZhHtml: 但这让情况变得更糟，因为瑞士的大部分地区都是完全无人居住的山区，而数百万人居住在堪萨斯州和内布拉斯加州等地，并希望能够接入互联网。

children.author: mlrtime
children.textRawHtml: And they do have it, but it does not need to be 25 gbps. Even if they had access to it, they wouldn't/shouldn't pay for it.
children.textZhHtml: 他们确实有，但不需要是 25 Gbps。即使他们有权访问它，他们也不会/不应该为此付费。

children.author: timcambrant
children.textRawHtml:
25 Gbps is a silly (but illustrative / clickbaity) take in this context. The more relevant measures as others have pointed out is how well-covered clusters of population are, but also what their broadband quality is. I live in Sweden, where we have extremely well-built out broadband networks, especially in cities. (My professional take is that quality is often very poor, but that is comparing to the rest of the country). We are a small country too, but we have still chosen symmetric active ethernet over the assymmetric PON, we have largely discontinued DSL, our cable/coax offerings have largely been replaced with fibre, and where DSL was not profitable, 4G is generally built out well enough to cover most people. Swedes in rural areas buy Starlink not for their reach, but for redundancy or even price.

I would say most of those things are not true in the US market. The united states IS a much, much bigger country, but my guess is that the median reach, stability, and speed is way lower than the Swedish and Swiss examples, compared to what it could be. The US has way higher military costs, still relatively high taxes, and most of all a stronger buying power. I pay $35/month for 300/300 Mbps Active Ethernet FTTH, and consider that overprized. When I last looked, the median American could generally afford more than that, but had access to less. Sorry, I don't have the numbers at hand.
children.textZhHtml:
在这个语境下，25 Gbps的说法很荒谬（但很能说明问题/很吸引眼球）。正如其他人指出的，更相关的衡量标准是人口聚集区的覆盖程度，以及他们的宽带质量。我住在瑞典，我们的宽带网络建设得极其完善，尤其是在城市。（从专业角度来看，质量往往很差，但那是和全国其他地区相比）。我们也是个小国，但我们仍然选择了非对称PON之外的对称有源以太网，我们基本淘汰了DSL，我们的同轴电缆服务也大多被光纤取代，在DSL无利可图的地方，4G网络的建设通常也足以覆盖大多数人。农村地区的瑞典人购买Starlink不是为了它的覆盖范围，而是为了冗余甚至价格。

我认为这些情况在美国市场大多不成立。美国确实是一个大得多的国家，但我猜，与应有的水平相比，其中位覆盖范围、稳定性和速度远低于瑞典和瑞士的例子。美国的军费开支高得多，税收仍然相对较高，最重要的是购买力更强。我每月支付35美元使用300/300 Mbps的有源以太网FTTH，还觉得这价格太高了。上次我查的时候，美国中位数收入的人通常能负担得起比这更高的费用，但享受到的服务却更少。抱歉，我手头没有具体数据。

children.author: TheScaryOne
children.textRawHtml:
My 8mbps DSL line is $43USD/month after everything, and the cheapest internet I have access to.

The next tier up is Starlink or 250mbps Fiber, which have the same cost. I do not wish to support Starlink. But I also can't afford to almost double my internet bill.

I live on a major national highway in a crossroads town.
children.textZhHtml:
算上所有费用，我的8mbps DSL线路每月要43美元，这已经是我能用的最便宜的网络了。

下一个档次是Starlink或250mbps光纤，价格一样。我不想支持Starlink。但我也负担不起几乎翻倍的网费。

我住在一个十字路口小镇的国家级主干公路旁。

children.author: AnthonyMouse
children.textRawHtml:
Which of the things you said don't also apply to the people in Zurich?

Also, if you say people in Kansas don't need fiber then you have to expect that to get averaged in. The population density in California is less than half what it is in Switzerland. Only 10% of US states have a higher population density than Switzerland and those states represent less than 10% of the US population (because of those 5 states the one with the largest land area is Maryland).
children.textZhHtml:
你说的这些情况，哪一条不适用于苏黎世的人呢？

另外，如果你说堪萨斯州的人不需要光纤，那你就得预料到这会拉低平均水平。加利福尼亚州的人口密度还不到瑞士的一半。只有10%的美国州人口密度高于瑞士，而这些州的人口仅占美国总人口的不到10%（由于这5个州的存在，陆地面积最大的州是马里兰州）。

children.author: dlcarrier
children.textRawHtml:
If the least populated 3% of Switzerland's geographic area didn't have internet access at all, no one would care because it's just a single frozen mountain.

If the least populated 3% of the USA's geographic area didn't have internet access at all, people would care because it's the entire state of Wyoming. Okay, most people wouldn't care, but some people in Wyoming would.
children.textZhHtml:
如果瑞士人口最少的 3% 的地理区域根本没有互联网接入，那么没有人会关心，因为它只是一座冰冻的山。

如果美国人口最少的 3% 的地理区域根本没有互联网接入，人们会关心，因为这是整个怀俄明州。 好吧，大多数人都不会关心，但怀俄明州的一些人会关心。

children.author: boxed
children.textRawHtml: That seems like another way to lie with silly stats though. If 3% of the least populated areas of all the subdivisions of the US the size of Switzerland were not connected, then that would indeed be just as irrelevant.
children.textZhHtml: 不过，这似乎是用愚蠢的统计数据撒谎的另一种方式。如果瑞士大小的美国所有分区中的 3% 的人口最少地区没有相连，那么这确实是无关紧要的。

children.author: mrheosuper
children.textRawHtml:
> A countries size doesn't matter since twice the area will, all things being equal

It will never be both objectively and subjectively equal. Even the geographic is already difference, the weather, then there is people, wealth, etc.
children.textZhHtml:
> 一个国家的大小并不重要，因为在所有条件都相同的情况下，国家面积的两倍会带来影响

它永远不会在客观和主观上都平等。连地理都已经不同了，天气，还有人，财富等等。

children.author: Turskarama
children.textRawHtml: Yes but all of those things are independent of the size of the country, which is the point.
children.textZhHtml: 是的，但所有这些事情都与国家的大小无关，这就是重点。

children.author: baybal2
children.textRawHtml: [dead]
children.textZhHtml: [已死]

children.author: MemoryHoleHQ
children.textRawHtml: [dead]
children.textZhHtml: [已死]

children.author: colechristensen
children.textRawHtml: Wrong, there are scaling problems.  Have you ever worked at both a very large and very small company?  Were things that were easy at small companies much harder at large ones?
children.textZhHtml: 错误，存在扩展问题。  您是否曾经在一家非常大和非常小的公司工作过？  在小公司容易做的事情在大公司就更难吗？

children.author: taveras
children.textRawHtml: It's a false equivalence to compare working in a company to have the same scaling problems as implementing policies to set up broadband infrastructure.
children.textZhHtml: 将在公司工作与实施建立宽带基础设施的政策所面临的扩展问题进行比较是错误的。

children.author: mlrtime
children.textRawHtml:
Not really at all.

Taking politics out of it. If you wanted to start a 25gbps project today, who do you think could get it done quicker. A small local municipality with a few network engineers. Or getting a Federal agency to do it?

My money is on the municipality.
children.textZhHtml:
一点也不。

排除政治因素。如果您今天想启动一个 25Gbps 项目，您认为谁可以更快地完成它。一个只有几名网络工程师的地方小市镇。或者让联邦机构来做这件事？

我的钱在市政府身上。

children.author: taveras
children.textRawHtml:
I agree that a smaller municipality would be more successful, but not because they are smaller.

The municipality is a more concentrated interest, and more motivated to deliver on a solution that works for their constituents.

Compare that to the federal government needing to execute a national “Interstate Highway for broadband” mega-project. The federal government can and has executed these kinds of projects, but it needs to consider different things than the municipalities.

Extending the highway metaphor, a smaller municipality can pave local roads just as quickly as the federal government can build highways.

Efficiency is not a perfect function of government size.
children.textZhHtml:
我同意规模较小的市政府可能会更成功，但这并不是因为它们规模小。

市政府的利益更加集中，也更有动力为选民提供切实可行的解决方案。

相比之下，联邦政府需要执行全国性的“宽带州际公路”超级项目。联邦政府能够且确实执行过这类项目，但它需要考虑的因素与市政府不同。

沿用公路的比喻，较小的市政府铺设本地道路的速度，完全可以和联邦政府修建高速公路的速度一样快。

效率并非政府规模的绝对函数。

children.author: matt-p
children.textRawHtml: So why don't you have ISPs that focus on one state or one geographical area. We have that all over in Europe.
children.textZhHtml: 那么为什么没有专注于一个州或一个地理区域的 ISP。我们在欧洲到处都是这样的。

stories.title: For first time, a cell built from scratch grows and divides
stories.titleZh: 第一次，从头开始构建的细胞生长并分裂

comments.author: JumpCrisscross
comments.textRawHtml:
“This was where the field had been stuck for some time. Researchers before Adamala had figured out different ways to feed and grow synthetic cells and to replicate their DNA. But cell division is a different beast. A typical cell reorganizes its cytoskeleton — a network of protein fibers that provide structural support — to halve its DNA and split. Synthetic biologists could not figure out how to get their cells to undergo this complex process.

So Adamala decided to ditch the cytoskeleton. One day, while tearing through the literature, she came across an interesting mechanism in a paper (opens a new tab). By attaching protein tags to a cell membrane, the synthetic biologist Reinhard Lipowsky (opens a new tab) at the Max Planck Institute of Colloids and Interfaces attracted other proteins to crowd around and physically bend the membrane, forcing the cell to divide. Following this approach, Adamala tweaked a cell-membrane protein and tested it in her protocells. After several tries, it worked.“

This is the novel bit.
comments.textZhHtml:
“这正是该领域停滞不前的一段时间。在Adamala之前，研究人员已经找到了喂养和培育合成细胞以及复制其DNA的不同方法。但细胞分裂完全是另一回事。典型的细胞会重组其细胞骨架——一种提供结构支持的蛋白纤维网络——以使DNA减半并分裂。合成生物学家一直无法弄清楚如何让他们的细胞经历这一复杂过程。

于是Adamala决定放弃细胞骨架。有一天，她在大量阅读文献时，偶然发现了一篇论文中一个有趣的机制（打开新标签页）。马克斯·普朗克胶体与界面研究所的合成生物学家Reinhard Lipowsky（打开新标签页）通过在细胞膜上附着蛋白质标签，吸引其他蛋白质聚集在周围并物理性地弯曲细胞膜，从而迫使细胞分裂。遵循这一方法，Adamala对一种细胞膜蛋白进行了微调，并在她的原始细胞中进行了测试。经过几次尝试，终于成功了。”

这就是新颖之处。

children.author: dnautics
children.textRawHtml: without criticising the work (its very cool and a very important first step) they haven't figured out division yet, which is kind of important.

My paper demonstrating a side channel attack on RSA via hyperthreading was rejected from the crypto preprint archive on the basis that it was "not cryptography".

(Reviewers at J.Crypto subsequently sat on it for a year and then suggested I submit it to a journal on CPU microarchitecture instead.)

Novel research is uniquely susceptible to "cool but it's not part of our field", because that critique is entirely correct until the research gets published!

（《密码学杂志》的审稿人随后把这篇论文搁置了一年，然后建议我改投一本关于CPU微架构的期刊。）

新颖的研究特别容易遭到“很酷，但这不属于我们的领域”这种评价，因为在研究成果发表之前，这种批评完全正确！

comments.author: Mg6yDfjp5U
comments.textRawHtml:
I recently left Google having worked on a number of projects with various YouTube teams. I think I can explain why it's being handled this way by YouTube.

This is a fairly nuanced/involved issue, so the task of classifying the bug likely made it's way to one of the engineers responsible for the implementation of this feature.

That engineer has already launched this project, and filed it away under their GRAD (performance) artifacts for when promo/annual review talks roll around. There's no motivation for this engineer to waste time fixing this bug because it won't benefit their promo packet, and they are already being put under pressure to launch other projects which _will_ benefit their promo packet.

So they do what they can to sweep it under the rug because that's what the promo/annual review framework (GRAD) incentivizes and rewards.
comments.textZhHtml:
我最近离开 Google，与多个 YouTube 团队一起参与了多个项目。我想我可以解释为什么 YouTube 会这样处理。

这是一个相当微妙/涉及的问题，因此对错误进行分类的任务可能会交给负责实现此功能的工程师之一。

该工程师已经启动了这个项目，并将其归档在他们的 GRAD（性能）工件下，以便在促销/年度审查会谈进行时使用。这位工程师没有动力浪费时间修复这个错误，因为这对他们的促销包没有好处，而且他们已经面临启动其他项目的压力，这些项目_将_有利于他们的促销包。

因此，他们尽其所能将其掩盖起来，因为这就是促销/年度审查框架（GRAD）的激励和奖励。

children.author: brailsafe
children.textRawHtml:
> This is a prime example of why programmers are not seriously considered engineers.

Seems to me like your comment is simply an example of prejudice.

You're just describing another standardized incentive structure that you're operating in, and using that as a basis to extrapolate that programmers of all kinds—whether they work on a video platform or on machinery that could cause catastrophe if it fails—are implicitly careless careerists who refuse responsibility by nature.
children.textZhHtml:
> 这是为什么程序员不被认真视为工程师的一个典型例子。

在我看来，你的评论只是一个偏见的例子。

你只是描述了你正在使用的另一种标准化激励结构，并以此为基础来推断各种程序员——无论他们在视频平台上工作，还是在一旦失败可能导致灾难的机器上工作——都是隐含的粗心的野心家，拒绝承担责任本质上。

children.author: digdugdirk
children.textRawHtml:
Other fields of engineering usually have a regulated licensure, upon which they can call themselves a Professional Engineer. This gives them the ability to make final approval/sign-off on designs and technical reports. It's most common in civil engineering, where a PE license is required for all publicly funded projects (and most privately funded ones as well, due to local/regional/national regulations) to be approved.

This license requires the holder to uphold code of professional ethics, and makes the engineer themselves be personally responsible for the safety and viability of the design itself. Losing a PE license is rare, but it does happen. The industry board (usually a regional board) can also discipline/reprimand engineers who fail to meet the professional standard - rubber stamping projects, personal misconduct, etc. Losing a license is a huge deal, but even reprimands can have a serious negative impact on someone's career.

In the industry the previous commenter works in their hypothetical would absolutely meet the bar for discipline or reprimand.
children.textZhHtml:
其他工程领域通常拥有受监管的许可证，据此他们可以称自己为专业工程师。这使他们能够对设计和技术报告进行最终批准/签署。这种情况在土木工程中最为常见，所有公共资助的项目（以及大多数私人资助的项目，由于当地/地区/国家法规的规定，也需要 PE 许可证）才能获得批准。

该许可证要求持有者遵守职业道德准则，并要求工程师本人对设计本身的安全性和可行性负责。失去 PE 许可证的情况很少见，但确实会发生。行业委员会（通常是地区委员会）还可以对不符合专业标准的工程师进行纪律处分/谴责——橡皮图章项目、个人不当行为等。失去许可证是一件大事，但即使是谴责也会对某人的职业生涯产生严重的负面影响。

在行业中，之前的评论者在他们的假设中工作绝对符合纪律或谴责的标准。

children.author: AnthonyMouse
children.textRawHtml:
The incentive structure you're describing is also a major contributor to cost disease.

Every decision to increase the cost of a product is taking that money out of the customer's pocket which they then can't use to buy more nutritious food or medicine or make rent and avoid becoming homeless. Every additional tax dollar spent on inflating the cost of an infrastructure project is one that can't be spent on cancer research or Pell grants or catching pedos. Moreover, that type of "tax" is highly regressive because when you make e.g. housing cost more, only the poor become unable to afford it.

Meanwhile the system you're referring to gives the engineers the incentive to be excessively risk-averse. Give someone the authority to command that resources be allocated to something and liability for not allocating them but no liability for what happens to the people the resources were allocated from and the result is not an optimal system.
children.textZhHtml:
你所描述的激励结构也是导致成本病的主要原因。

每一个增加产品成本的决定，都是从客户的口袋里掏钱，而他们本可以用这些钱购买更有营养的食物、药品，或者支付房租以避免流落街头。每多花一美元税款来推高基础设施项目的成本，就少了一美元用于癌症研究、佩尔助学金或抓捕恋童癖。此外，这种“税”具有高度的累退性质，因为当你让例如住房成本增加时，只有穷人会负担不起。

同时，你所指的这种系统给了工程师过度规避风险的动机。赋予某人指挥资源分配的权力，并对不分配资源承担责任，却对资源被抽走的人所遭遇的后果不负任何责任，这样产生的结果绝不是一个最优系统。

children.author: miki123211
children.textRawHtml:
The problem with software is that software doesn't care about where it's built. Jurisdictions need to balance safety and quality regulation with the fact that you can just make the software somewhere where the regulations aren't so onerous, and most software is made very far from the place where it is used.

If you're making a bridge usable by residents of Springfield, that bridge has to be in Springfield, and it has to be made by Springfield engineers following Springfield laws.
children.textZhHtml:
软件的问题在于，软件并不关心它是在哪里开发的。司法管辖区需要在安全与质量监管之间取得平衡，同时考虑到你完全可以在监管不那么繁重的地方开发软件，而且大多数软件的开发地与其使用地相距甚远。

如果你要建造一座供斯普林菲尔德居民使用的桥梁，那座桥就必须建在斯普林菲尔德，并且必须由斯普林菲尔德的工程师按照斯普林菲尔德的法律来建造。

children.author: jorvi

It is up to us to also make use of them. Someone who graduated from a bootcamp likely will not have the tools to actually engineer this stuff, unless they got some prior experience in engineering.

> Spain is “making a big mistake,” said Bart Groothuis [...] “Spain is now dependent on the country with the largest and most sophisticated offensive espionage program directed against us.”

I highly doubt he's naive enough to believe the "against us" qualifier exempts the operator of the largest and most sophisticated offensive espionage program ever.
children.textZhHtml:
这确实令人不安，但下面这段话让我笑出了声：

> 巴特·格罗特胡伊斯表示，西班牙“犯了一个大错”……“西班牙现在依赖于那个对我们发动最大规模、最复杂进攻性间谍活动的国家。”

我严重怀疑他是否天真到相信“针对我们”这个限定词能让有史以来最大规模、最复杂的进攻性间谍活动的实施者免责。

children.author: danudey
children.textRawHtml:
The reason everyone hates them is that they've been committing genocide for decades and have refused any sort of peace process. All the countries around them have offered to guarantee Israel's security in exchange for just letting the Palestians live their lives free of oppression but Israel is not willing to give up its land grab because the narrative that 'everyone is trying to destroy us' is what keeps the right wing in power and allows the population to look the other way.

Even the US was concerned recently that Israel was planning not just to sabotage the peace talks with Iran but to assassinate the Iranian representatives:

https://www.israelnationalnews.com/news/429561

The only reason Israel has its back to the wall is that its 'never again' attitude has extended to any concept of solving problems that doesn't involve military force, and since they've had the backing of the US that means they've been able to get away with anything they wanted so far. It's the asshole little brother whose big brother will beat you up if he ever runs the risk of facing the consequences of his actions.

If Israel had any desire to live in peace with their neighbors then maybe they would, but either they don't want peace except through victory, they don't want peace if it means giving up the ability to continue their ethnic cleansing of Palestine to build more settlements, or they want peace but they don't believe that anyone else wants it (despite everyone else trying to work towards it except them).

If they hadn't been so focused on aggression, genocide, and apartheid for the past 70 years maybe this all could have been solved, but the narrative that they're the only bastion of democracy and freedom in West Asia/North Africa and that's why everyone wants to destroy them doesn't hold up to the barest of scrutiny.
children.textZhHtml:
大家恨他们的原因在于，他们几十年来一直在实施种族灭绝，并拒绝任何形式的和平进程。他们周围的所有国家都曾提出保障以色列的安全，条件仅仅是让巴勒斯坦人免受压迫地生活，但以色列不愿放弃其掠夺土地的行径，因为“所有人都想毁灭我们”这种说辞正是右翼维持执政地位、让民众对一切视而不见的借口。

甚至美国最近也担心，以色列不仅计划破坏与伊朗的和平谈判，还企图暗杀伊朗代表：

https://www.israelnationalnews.com/news/429561

以色列之所以陷入绝境，唯一的原因就是他们把“绝不让悲剧重演”的态度泛化到了任何非军事手段解决问题的理念上，而且仗着有美国撑腰，他们迄今为止得以肆意妄为、逃避一切后果。这就好比一个混蛋小弟弟，仗着大哥会替你出头，有恃无恐地胡作非为。

如果以色列真心想与邻国和平共处，那也许他们能做到，但他们要么只想要通过胜利换来的和平，要么如果和平意味着放弃继续对巴勒斯坦进行种族清洗以建立更多定居点的能力，他们就不想要和平，又或者他们想要和平，却不相信其他人也想要和平（尽管除了他们之外，所有人都在努力推动和平）。

如果过去70年里他们没有那么热衷于侵略、种族灭绝和种族隔离，也许这一切早就解决了，但他们那种自诩为西亚/北非唯一民主自由堡垒、因此所有人都想毁灭他们的说辞，根本经不起哪怕是最基本的推敲。

children.author: tancop
children.textRawHtml:
china has no political agenda outside of their country (that includes taiwan from their pov). all they care about is staying in power at home and keeping up the economy. they only sell to russia because its a good way to make money.

belt and road, deals with african dictators, selling cars and phones to europe, temu and alibaba, open source llms, all of that is for higher tax revenue. a lot of chinese companies are part state owned so thats another way it directly benefits the government.

even the illegal police stations only exist to make sure overseas chinese communities dont support opposition movements back home. is it a good thing? definitely not, but its also not a threat to the host country as a whole.

the spying they do is either economic espionage thats good for the world (trade secrets should not exist, thats what patents are for) or good old state on state action like everybody else is doing. i bet russia and america each have more spies in europe than china.
children.textZhHtml:
中国在境外没有任何政治议程（从他们的视角来看，这也包括台湾）。他们关心的只是在国内维持执政地位和发展经济。他们之所以向俄罗斯出售商品，只是因为这是赚钱的好办法。

一带一路、与非洲独裁者做交易、向欧洲出售汽车和手机、Temu和阿里巴巴、开源大语言模型，所有这些都是为了增加税收。很多中国企业是国有或国有控股的，所以这也是直接惠及政府的另一种方式。

就连那些非法警务站的存在，也只是为了确保海外华人社区不支持国内的反对运动。这是好事吗？绝对不是，但这对整个东道国也构不成威胁。

他们搞的间谍活动，要么是造福世界的经济间谍活动（商业秘密本就不该存在，那是专利该干的事），要么就是像其他国家一样搞的老一套国家间情报行动。我敢打赌，俄罗斯和美国在欧洲的间谍都比中国多。
